---
title: "My Showcase"
description: "A short showcase of one my data visualisations and what it tells us."
output: distill::distill_article
---


### What this is
This page showcases pieces of work from GV300. I present a visualisation, the code used to generate it, and my interpretation.


### The question
From GV300 Homework 3: This analysis examines whether an issue-focused homepage increases the probability that visitors sign up to support a political party.


### The data
I use simulated A/B test data in which website visitors are randomly assigned to either a standard homepage (control) or an issue-focused version (treatment). The outcome variable, support_signup, equals 1 if the visitor signs up and 0 otherwise.


### Visualisation
```{r plot-signup-rates, fig.width=7, fig.height=4}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)

# Setup
# Simulate the A/B test data (from Assignment 3)
n <- 1500
treatment <- rbinom(n = n, size = 1, prob = 0.5)

p_control <- 0.12
p_treated <- 0.17

Y0 <- rbinom(n = n, size = 1, prob = p_control)
Y1 <- rbinom(n = n, size = 1, prob = p_treated)

support_signup <- ifelse(treatment == 1, Y1, Y0)

party_ab <- data.frame(
  visitor_id = 1:n,
  treatment = treatment,
  support_signup = support_signup
)

# --- Group means + ATE ---
means_df <- party_ab %>%
  mutate(treatment = ifelse(treatment == 0,
                            "Control homepage",
                            "Issue-focused homepage")) %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(mean_signup = mean(support_signup), .groups = "drop")

mean_control <- means_df$mean_signup[means_df$treatment == "Control homepage"]
mean_treated <- means_df$mean_signup[means_df$treatment == "Issue-focused homepage"]
ate_hat <- mean_treated - mean_control

# Print key numbers
cat("Control signup rate:", percent(mean_control, accuracy = 0.1), "\n")
cat("Treatment signup rate:", percent(mean_treated, accuracy = 0.1), "\n")
cat("ATE:", round(ate_hat * 100, 2), "percentage points\n")


# Plot
ggplot(means_df, aes(x = treatment, y = mean_signup, fill = treatment)) +
geom_col(width = 0.6) +
geom_text(
aes(label = paste0(round(mean_signup * 100, 1), "%")),
vjust = -0.5
) +
scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.25), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control homepage" = "gray70",
"Issue-focused homepage" = "steelblue")) +
labs(
x = NULL,
y = "Signup rate",
title = "Supporter signup rate by homepage version"
) +
theme_minimal() +
theme(legend.position = "none")

```


### Interpretation
The figure shows that visitors exposed to the issue-focused homepage were more likely to sign up than those who saw the standard version. The signup rate increases from roughly 11.9% in the control group to about 16.0% in the treatment group, implying an average treatment effect of around 4.2 percentage points. Because visitors were randomly assigned to homepage versions, this difference can be interpreted as the causal effect of the issue-focused messaging. Substantively, the results suggest that emphasising a salient political issue can modestly improve supporter conversion on the party’s website.


### Limitation
However, the data are simulated and therefore may not fully capture the complexity of real-world campaign behaviour. In practice, user responses to political messaging may depend on many additional factors such as prior partisanship, message saturation, or strategic targeting. The external validity of the findings should therefore be interpreted with caution.

The full code for this analysis is available on my GitHub repository:
https://github.com/adamster08/GV300-Portfolio-2507416
